what_it_does: This file is a ledger record describing a design-assets icon image named "image (32)", stored as a PNG blob, with version, authorship, and addressing metadata.
observations: The asset is a PNG referenced by blob://b2:fe66104c8abe9c05c36eaca8e27ece01
Recorded width and height are both 0 despite the "(32)" name suggesting a 32px icon
The alt text in version 1 is empty
There are no links to or from this record
created and updated timestamps are identical
The id, address, path, and filename contain spaces and parentheses
standouts: Width and height are 0 for an icon whose name implies 32px
No links or relationships are recorded
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter inside a Markdown file for ledger metadata
Uses channel-memory path naming with __ separators and spaces
Includes a versions list even when only one version exists
concerns: w: 0 and h: 0 may indicate a metadata extraction failure or placeholder
Spaces and parentheses in identifiers may cause URL or tooling fragility
notes: A ledger stub for a 32px design-assets icon whose metadata currently reports zero dimensions and no alt text.
questions: Why are the width and height recorded as 0?
Is the empty alt text intentional or missing?
purpose_vs_actual: The file appears intended to catalog a 32px icon, but the recorded width and height are 0, so the metadata does not currently reflect the apparent size.
intention_signals: It seems to be trying to register a small PNG icon in the design-assets channel so it can be addressed, versioned, and retrieved by other parts of the system.
contribution: Provides a versioned, addressable metadata record for a PNG icon asset, including blob reference, channel, path, authorship session, and timestamps.
summary_for_embedding: A ledger metadata record for a PNG icon named "image (32)" in the design-assets channel, referencing blob b2:fe66104c8abe9c05c36eaca8e27ece01 with zero recorded dimensions and empty alt text.
intention_for_embedding: To catalog a 32px design-assets icon image with a versioned blob reference for retrieval and tracking.
